qa_pairs.question: What are the primary evaluation criteria and their corresponding weights for ranking different GPU models into Reference GPU Units (RGUs)?
qa_pairs.answer: The primary evaluation criteria for ranking different GPU models into Reference GPU Units (RGUs) are the FP32 score (with dense matrices on regular GPU cores) at 40%, the FP16 score (with dense matrices on Tensor cores) also at 40%, and the GPU memory score at 20%.

qa_pairs.question: What user usage patterns influenced the selection of the evaluation criteria for Reference GPU Units (RGUs)?
qa_pairs.answer: The evaluation criteria were chosen because roughly half of the users primarily utilize single-precision floating-point operations (FP32), the other half use half-precision floating-point operations (FP16), and a significant portion of all users are constrained by the amount of memory on the GPU.
